dashboards.description: Executive-level learning program overview combining learner volume, cost tracking, compliance donut, ILT operations, and course popularity. Three-column layout with metric panels, compliance pie chart, and instructor-led training KPI grid.
dashboards.layout_pattern: 3-column: Learning Metrics | Compliance Donut | ILT Grid + Bottom Row: Job Bar | Top Courses Table | YOY Line

insights.name: High Repeat Learner Rate
insights.description: 60% of learners (900/1500) are repeat users, indicating strong platform stickiness but potential content gap for new learner acquisition.

insights.name: Compliance Assignment Backlog
insights.description: Assigned status dominates the compliance donut (~70%), with small completed/in-progress slices suggesting most assigned training has not been started.

insights.name: ILT Attendance Below Target
insights.description: 73% attendance rate against 15-seat average capacity means ~4 empty seats per session, representing wasted ILT investment.

insights.name: Sharp Course Usage Decline in 2017
insights.description: Courses used dropped from ~2200 (2016) to ~1100 (2017), a 50% decline suggesting platform migration, content retirement, or engagement drop.

dashboards.description: Real-time and trending login engagement metrics showing daily/weekly/monthly login volumes, job-role login distribution, year-over-year trends, and recent login audit trail with employee details.
dashboards.layout_pattern: Top Row: 6 Colored KPI Badges | Mid Row: Job Table | YOY Line | 4-Week Area | Bottom: Recent Logins Detail Table

insights.name: High Session-to-User Ratio
insights.description: 31 logins from only 5 unique users today suggests power users generating multiple sessions — potential SSO timeout or session management issue.

insights.name: Low Monthly Unique Penetration
insights.description: Only 51 unique logins this month against 3335 total sessions indicates a small active user base with heavy repeat access.

insights.name: Service Managers Dominate Engagement
insights.description: Service Managers account for 145 of ~438 weekly logins (33%), disproportionately high compared to other roles.

dashboards.description: Comprehensive L&D operations view with training volume, cost, vendor analysis, activity type breakdown, and monthly/organizational completion tracking across top 10 organizations and vendors.
dashboards.layout_pattern: Top: 6-Cell KPI Strip | Mid: 3x3 Grid (Org Completions, Org Cancellations, Age Pie, Future Regs, Vendor Hours) | Bottom: Activity Types, Monthly Hours, Vendor Spend

metrics.name: Completed Trainings per Organization - Top 10
metrics.section: No. of Completed Trainings per organization - Top 10

metrics.name: Cancelled Trainings per Organization - Top 10
metrics.section: No. of cancelled training per organization - Top 10

metrics.name: Completed Training Hours by Age Category
metrics.section: Completed Training Hours By Age Category

metrics.name: Future Registrations per Organization - Top 10
metrics.section: No. of Future Registrations per organization - Top 10

metrics.name: Top 10 Vendors by Amount of Hours
metrics.section: Top 10 Vendors by Amount of Hours

insights.name: Cancellation Rate Analysis
insights.description: 567 cancellations against 27,254 completions yields a ~2% cancellation rate. However, 'Managing Cross' has 144 cancellations — a disproportionate outlier needing investigation.

insights.name: Session Activity Type Dominates Hours
insights.description: Sessions account for 2,499 hours — nearly 4x the next highest activity type, suggesting heavy reliance on live/virtual session delivery.

insights.name: Vendor Concentration Risk - Hooli
insights.description: Hooli dominates vendor hours (2,499) and spend ($48,546), creating single-vendor dependency risk for a significant portion of L&D delivery.

insights.name: Strong Q4 Seasonality in Training Hours
insights.description: Oct-Nov peak (110K-140K hours) with mid-year trough (May-Jul at 3.5K-15K) indicates heavy end-of-year compliance push or budget cycle alignment.

insights.name: 30-39 Age Group Training Concentration
insights.description: The 30-39 age bracket consumes ~45% of completed training hours. Under-25 and over-50 groups show minimal engagement, potentially missing early-career and late-career development.

dashboards.description: Dark-themed development plan tracking view showing plan duration, goal/activity counts, completion rates by goal category (Leadership, Communication, Listening, Development), and per-employee completion detail with planned vs actual start dates.
dashboards.layout_pattern: Top: KPI Cards (4) + Goal Completion Bar + Completion Donut | Bottom: Employee Detail Table

metrics.name: Overall Completion Rate
metrics.description: Approximately 60-65% completed based on donut visualization

insights.name: Development Goal Lagging Behind
insights.description: The 'Development' goal has only 1 of 3 completed vs Leadership Training at 3 of 4 — indicating the broadest goal category has the least completion traction.

insights.name: Zero-Completion Employees at Risk
insights.description: Erwin Schroedinger shows 0% completion despite starting on time (Sep 04 planned, Oct 04 actual). Late start of 1 month with zero progress needs intervention.

insights.name: Consistent Start Date Delays
insights.description: Multiple employees show 1-month delays between planned and actual start dates, suggesting systemic onboarding or scheduling friction in the development plan rollout.

dashboards.description: Curriculum-level analytics showing child activity metrics, monthly completion trends with seasonal patterns, year-over-year completion growth, score distributions, and per-activity detail with registration/completion/in-progress breakdowns.
dashboards.layout_pattern: Top: KPI Cards (3) + Completion Monthly Area + YOY Line | Mid: Score Distribution Curve + Activity Type Bar | Bottom: Activity Detail Table

insights.name: Score Distribution Heavily Right-Skewed
insights.description: Average score of 97.33 with peak at 98 suggests assessments may be too easy or content is well-calibrated. Very few scores below 89.

insights.name: Completions Heavily Front-Loaded
insights.description: Jan-Feb account for ~80% of annual completions, with near-zero activity Jun-Dec. Suggests a one-time push or annual compliance deadline in Q1.

insights.name: Massive YOY Completion Growth
insights.description: Completions grew from ~10 (2016) to ~260 (2017), a 26x increase indicating either new curriculum launch or expanded audience rollout in 2017.

insights.name: Decisiveness Course Below Target
insights.description: Decisiveness has only 50% completion rate vs 100% for Developing People and Negotiating — may need engagement intervention or content review.

dashboards.description: Individual learner profile view (dark theme) showing employee details, activity status breakdown (Attended/Cancelled/In-Progress/No-Show), assignment vs completion counts, training hours, and year-over-year activity and training hour trends.
dashboards.layout_pattern: Top: Learner Detail Card + Activity Status Bar + KPI Quad | Bottom: Activity Type Bar + YOY Launch/Completion Line + YOY Hours Bar

insights.name: High No-Show Rate
insights.description: 18 no-shows against 25 attended (42% no-show rate) is critically high and wastes ILT/session capacity. Requires manager intervention.

insights.name: Self-Driven Learner Pattern
insights.description: 20 completions vs only 4 assigned indicates the learner is primarily self-directed, completing 5x more activities than formally assigned.

insights.name: Digital Learning Preference
insights.description: CBT Course (12) and WBT Course (12) dominate activity types, showing strong preference for computer/web-based self-paced learning over ILT.

dashboards.description: Team-level compliance and training status view with assigned/completed/yet-to-start/overdue counts, training status distribution, compliance vs non-compliance trends, score distribution histogram, and compliance training year-over-year bars.
dashboards.layout_pattern: Top: KPI Quad (Assigned/Completed/Yet to Start/Overdue) + Training Status Bar + Compliance Trend | Bottom: Score Distribution Histogram + Compliance Training YOY Bar

metrics.name: Score Distribution
metrics.description: Bell-curve distribution centered around 75-85 with peak at ~80, range 15-100

insights.name: 45% Completion Rate
insights.description: Only 90 of 200 assigned trainings completed (45%). With 20 yet to start and 0 overdue, the remaining 90 are likely in-progress or status gap.

insights.name: Non-Compliance Approaching Compliance Levels
insights.description: Non-compliance trend line is converging with compliance completions (55 vs 60 in 2018), indicating the compliance gap is narrowing unfavorably.

insights.name: Healthy Score Distribution
insights.description: Normal bell curve centered at 75-85 suggests assessments are appropriately calibrated — neither too easy nor too hard for this team.

dashboards.description: Plan-level execution tracker with goal/activity counts, status distribution bar, direct assigns, audience size, completion percentage donut, and per-employee assignment detail table with status indicators (Completed/In-Progress/Overdue).
dashboards.layout_pattern: Top: Summary Cards (Goals/Activities) + Status Bar + Assigns/Audience + Completion Donut | Bottom: Employee Assign Detail Table

insights.name: Overdue Assignment - Maria Mitchell
insights.description: Maria Mitchell shows 0% completion with Overdue status and due date of Dec 18, 2017. Assigned 19 months ago with zero progress — requires immediate escalation.

insights.name: Extended Plan Duration
insights.description: 19-month assignment window (May 2016 to Dec 2017) is unusually long for 2 activities. Early completers (Jane Goodall - Jan 2017) finished in 8 months while others still incomplete.

insights.name: 50% Plan Completion at Deadline
insights.description: With the due date imminent (Dec 18, 2017), only 50% completion achieved. 3 employees still in-progress and 1 overdue.
